content:
Today I shared with you a practical story of how switching from readable JSON to compact binary serialization helped us reduce overhead, improve speed, and scale APIs without adding more servers. And why it's make you a superman to communicate API in microservice or API based solution for best traffic handling.

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why moving from human-readable JSON to compact binary serialization can dramatically improve API performance, reduce network overhead, and unlock higher system scalability.
JSON has become the default language of modern APIs.
It is simple, readable, flexible, and supported by almost every programming language, framework, browser, mobile app, and integration platform. For REST APIs, microservices, dashboards, mobile apps, and third-party integrations, JSON is often the first choice.
And for good reasons.
Developers can read it easily. Logs are understandable. Debugging is simple. Tools like Postman, Swagger, curl, browser DevTools, and API gateways handle JSON beautifully.
